name: Sweet-Sour Chicken a La Yan Can Cook
recipeIngredient: 1 lb Boneless chicken, cut into 1-inch cubes
1 tablespoon Xiaoxing rice wine or dry sherry
1/4 cup Cider vinegar (or rice vinegar or white vinegar)
1 Green bell pepper, cut into 3/4-inch squares
1 Red bell pepper, cut into 3/4-inch squares
1 can Lychee or Mandarin oranges (8-11 oz), drained
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix chicken, rice wine/sherry, soy sauce, and salt in a bowl. Let sit for at least 30 minutes.
2. Add the beaten egg to the mixture and stir well.
3. Combine cornstarch and flour (this creates a coating similar to water chestnut flour). Dredge chicken cubes in the mixture, shaking off excess.
4. Set aside for a few minutes before frying.
5. Heat oil in a deep pot or wok to 360°F (180°C) and fry chicken for 2 minutes (white meat) to 3 minutes (dark meat). Drain on paper towels and keep warm.
6. In the same pan, sauté ginger and garlic over high heat until fragrant.
7. Add the remaining sauce ingredients. Stir in the peppers, pineapple, and lychee/orange for 1 minute.
8. Mix cornstarch with cold water and stir into the sauce. Taste and adjust seasoning if needed (add more sugar, vinegar, or salt).
9. When the sauce thickens and becomes glossy, it is ready.
10. Reheat oil to 375°F (190°C) and briefly fry chicken for an additional 2 minutes until golden and crispy on the outside.
11. Add chicken to the sauce and vegetables, tossing well.
12. Serve with white rice.
